dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Track portfolio-level ESG signals, inspect recent uploads, and jump into company workflows without losing context.
dashboard.noCompanies: No companies yet. Upload a PDF report to get started.
dashboard.noReportsYet: No reports yet. Upload your first ESG report.
dashboard.chartsEmptyBody: Upload an ESG report to unlock trend charts and emission rankings.

upload.title: Upload ESG Report
upload.subtitle: Bring in annual reports or sustainability reports, keep the parsing workflow visible, and turn each file into a reusable company case rather than a one-off extraction.
upload.supportedHint: Best used for annual reports as the baseline, with sustainability reports and filings added later as supporting evidence.
upload.gapBannerBody: Upload a PDF for {{company}} ({{year}}) — the extraction will be stored against this company/year so the trend and framework views can pick it up.
upload.autoFetchDescription: Queue a background fetch attempt for this company/year. The result goes to pending_disclosures for review before merge.
upload.aiError: AI extraction failed — please check your API configuration
upload.pdfError: Could not extract text from this PDF. Please ensure it is not a scanned image.

taxonomy.title: EU Taxonomy Disclosure Mirror
taxonomy.subtitle: Review disclosed alignment ratios, DNSH signals, and objective-level evidence while tracking completeness and missing fields.
taxonomy.disclaimer: This view mirrors what the company disclosed against the EU Taxonomy framework and highlights missing fields. It is NOT a compliance verdict or audit opinion.
taxonomy.selectPrompt: Select a company above to view taxonomy analysis.

lcoe.title: LCOE Analysis
lcoe.subtitle: Model assumptions, validate units, and compare the economic profile of different clean-energy technologies in a way that feels like an analyst tool rather than a raw form.
lcoe.resultHintLcoe: Levelized cost output based on the current assumptions.
lcoe.resultHintNpv: Net present value under the current scenario.
lcoe.resultHintIrr: Internal rate of return derived from the modeled cash flows.
lcoe.resultHintPayback: Simple payback estimate for the scenario assumptions.
lcoe.validationTitle: Please fix the following assumptions before running the model:
lcoe.deMarketRefNote: Source: EPEX SPOT / Bundesnetzagentur SMARD. Not from company reports — set manually.
lcoe.cnMarketRefNote: Source: CEPCI / NEA annual data. Prices in CNY/MWh. Not from company reports — set manually.
lcoe.usMarketRefNote: Source: EIA wholesale electricity market data. Prices in USD/MWh. Not from company reports — set manually.
lcoe.fxToEur: FX rate to EUR (1 unit → EUR)
lcoe.fxNote: All inputs are in this currency. Converted to EUR for calculation and cross-region comparison.
lcoe.resultHintLcoeLocal: LCOE in input currency, for local market comparison.

validation.capacity: Capacity must be greater than 0 MW.
validation.capacityFactor: Capacity factor must be between 0 and 1.
validation.lifetime: Lifetime must be greater than 0 years.
validation.discountRate: Discount rate must be between 0 and 1.
validation.electricityPrice: Electricity selling price must be greater than 0 €/MWh.

fieldHelp.capacity_mw: Installed capacity of the project or asset fleet.
fieldHelp.capacity_factor: Expected utilization level as a ratio between 0 and 1.
fieldHelp.capex_eur_per_kw: Upfront investment intensity per kW installed.
fieldHelp.opex_eur_per_kw_year: Recurring annual operating cost per kW.
fieldHelp.lifetime_years: Economic lifetime used in the discounted cash-flow model.
fieldHelp.discount_rate: Discount factor used for the NPV and IRR assumptions.
fieldHelp.electricity_price_eur_per_mwh: Assumed market selling price for electricity. Used to compute NPV and IRR. Not extracted from company reports — must be set manually or via the Germany reference presets below.

projectAnalysis.modeBanner: Project Analysis Mode — inputs are user-provided technical parameters, not extracted from public disclosures.

companies.title: Companies
companies.subtitle: Review saved company cases as reusable analysis objects. Long names should stay readable, source types should stay visible, and core metrics should keep their units.
companies.companyCardHint: Open the company profile to inspect history, framework scores, and disclosure quality.

compare.title: Compare Companies
compare.subtitle: Lay multiple company cases side by side, keep units visible, and make it easier to explain differences across disclosure quality and performance.
compare.selectUp4: Select up to 4 companies to compare
compare.pickAtLeastTwo: This section becomes more useful once we add taxonomy objective charts or peer benchmarking. For now it confirms that the comparison layer can preserve units and readability.
compare.noSelection: Select companies above to compare metrics.
compare.noSelectionHeadline: Select two or more companies to begin
compare.noSelectionBullet2: Relative bars to spot the best and worst performer
compare.noSelectionBullet3: Switch between absolute values, intensity, and rank views

tooltips.scope1: Scope 1 — Direct greenhouse gas emissions from sources owned or controlled by the company (e.g. fuel combustion in boilers, company vehicles). Lower is better.
tooltips.scope2: Scope 2 — Indirect emissions from purchased electricity, heat, or steam. Reducing this typically means switching to renewable energy. Lower is better.
tooltips.scope3: Scope 3 — All other indirect emissions in the value chain (supply chain, product use, business travel). The largest category for most companies. Lower is better.
tooltips.revenueAligned: EU Taxonomy Alignment — share of total revenue from economic activities that meet EU sustainability criteria. Higher means more of the business is 'green' under European law.
tooltips.renewable: Renewable Energy Share — percentage of total energy consumption sourced from renewables (solar, wind, hydro, etc.). Higher is better.
tooltips.waterUsage: Total water withdrawal in cubic metres. Relevant for water-intensive industries. Lower is typically better, but context (water-stressed regions) matters.
tooltips.femalePct: Percentage of the total workforce that identifies as female. A diversity proxy. Higher values indicate better gender balance.
tooltips.intensity: Emissions intensity normalises Scope 1 CO₂ by total headcount, enabling fair size-adjusted comparison across companies of different scales.

benchmark.title: Industry Benchmarks
benchmark.subtitle: Cross-company disclosure percentiles by NACE industry
benchmark.disclaimer: Percentiles are computed from companies whose reports carry a NACE industry code. Results depend on sample size; p50 with n<5 is directional only.
benchmark.emptyMetrics: No benchmark data for this industry yet. Upload reports with this industry code, then click Recompute.
benchmark.emptyPeers: No companies tagged with this industry yet.
benchmark.lowSampleWarningTitle: Low sample size — treat these percentiles as directional only
benchmark.lowSampleWarningBody: The current selection includes sample sizes as low as {{count}}. Keep the benchmark table visible, but add caution when using p50/p75 values for decisions or external reporting.

peer.title: Peer comparison
peer.noIndustryCta: This company has no NACE industry code yet. Set it on the next upload to enable peer comparison.
peer.empty: No benchmark data available for this industry yet. Use the Industry Benchmarks page to recompute.
peer.disclaimer: Position is computed only when the company year and benchmark year match exactly. Buckets with sample size < 5 are directional only.
peer.yearMismatch: No exact benchmark year matches {{companyYear}}. Available benchmark years: {{benchmarkYears}}. No comparison is shown until the same year exists.

sourceTrail.title: Source trail ({{count}} runs)
sourceTrail.error: Could not load the source trail.
sourceTrail.unavailable: Source trail is unavailable for this record.

frameworks.title: Multi-Framework ESG
frameworks.subtitle: Compare EU Taxonomy 2020 · China CSRC 2023 · EU CSRD/ESRS simultaneously
frameworks.selectPrompt: Select a company to view tri-framework scoring and gap analysis.
frameworks.calculating: Calculating scores for all three frameworks…

profile.heroImprovingTitle: Disclosure readiness is moving in the right direction
profile.heroImprovingBody: Renewable share improved by {{renewableDelta}} while Scope 1 emissions moved down by {{scope1Delta}}. This is the strongest near-term signal on the page.
profile.heroDivergenceBody: Current framework spread is {{spread}}, which suggests the company looks materially different depending on the regulatory lens.
profile.heroCoverageTitle: The company profile is becoming reusable analysis infrastructure
profile.heroCoverageBody: This case already combines {{periods}} reporting periods with {{frameworks}} framework views, making it useful for repeat analysis instead of one-off reading.
profile.sourceMergePriorityReserved: Reserved for upcoming merge and source-priority explanation.
profile.radarLegend: Radar compares current overall score by framework
profile.trendLegend: Lines: Scope 1 (red), Renewable % (green), Taxonomy aligned revenue % (indigo)
profile.yoyScope1Hint: Negative values mean emissions decreased, which is usually positive.
profile.yoyTaxonomyHint: Tracks change in aligned revenue ratio where disclosed.
profile.yoyNarrativeReady: Compared with {{year}}, renewable share moved {{renewableDelta}} and taxonomy-aligned revenue moved {{taxonomyDelta}}. This is where the next version should explain the business or disclosure reason behind the change.
profile.yoyNarrativeMissing: There is not enough prior-period data yet to generate a strong year-over-year narrative.
profile.noFrameworkResults: No saved framework results yet. Run framework scoring first.
profile.dataQualityNoMissing: All tracked key disclosures are present.
profile.dataQualityMissingHint: Missing values are interpreted as not yet disclosed in the current case, not as a system error.
profile.whyIntro: This page is designed to prove that the product is more than a static ESG dashboard: it preserves time, framework, and evidence context in one reusable company view.
profile.whyPoint1Body: It shows that the product can translate public disclosures into multi-period, version-aware analysis instead of one-off extraction.
profile.whyPoint2Body: A client or analyst can see how readiness changes over time, not just what one report said in isolation.
profile.whyPoint3Body: The strongest long-term moat is not the chart itself, but the evidence-backed data structure and the ability to compare sources, periods, and frameworks.
profile.narrativeSnapshotBody: As of {{latestYear}}, this case consolidates {{periods}} reporting periods across {{years}} years and {{frameworks}} framework views. Current disclosure readiness is {{readiness}}.
profile.narrativeNoImprovement: No clear improvement signal is available from the current period-over-period data.
profile.narrativeNoWeakening: No clear weakening signal is visible in the current comparable metrics.
profile.narrativeNoStrengths: No stable disclosure strengths are identifiable yet.
profile.narrativeNoGaps: No key disclosure gaps are currently detected.
profile.narrativePortfolioTitle: Why this case is useful as a portfolio example
profile.narrativePortfolioBody: The CATL profile demonstrates a complete workflow: multi-period data continuity, cross-framework interpretation, and explicit disclosure strengths/gaps. With {{periods}} periods and {{frameworks}} framework views in one page, it is suitable for professional product and analyst demonstrations.

manual.title: Manual Input / Case Builder
manual.subtitle: Build reliable demo companies even when API quota is gone. Save structured company-year records locally, keep period context, and reuse them across profile and framework pages.
manual.storageHint: This page writes to your local backend database. Same company + year currently updates the existing record.
manual.metricsHint: Fill only the fields you already trust. Missing values stay explicit and can be backfilled later.
manual.overwriteHint: Current storage is still keyed by company + year, so using the same pair will update the existing record. That is acceptable for annual baseline cases and temporary showcase drafts.

regional.subtitle: EU · China · US regulatory framework alignment
regional.selectPrompt: Select a company to view three-region ESG comparison.
